commit_message: Try GELU activation instead of ReLU^2
description: replace F.relu(x).square() with F.gelu(x) in MLP
hypothesis: GELU is smoother and may converge better for depth-2 with ~170 steps; ReLU^2 is tuned for longer runs
next_move: if worse, ReLU^2 is better for this task; if better, consider SiLU as well

description: change MLP from 4x to 3x hidden dimension expansion
hypothesis: fewer MLP parameters = faster forward pass = more optimizer steps in 5 min — same logic that made depth=2 beat depth=3
next_move: if better, try 2x; if worse, 4x is right

hypothesis: no gradient clipping is currently applied; clipping at 1.0 may stabilize Adam param updates and improve convergence
next_move: if better, try tighter clip at 0.5; if worse, the model is stable enough without clipping

description: change HEAD_DIM from 128 to 64; model_dim becomes 192 with 3 attention heads
hypothesis: more attention heads with smaller dim may help the 2-layer model attend to more diverse patterns; model_dim shrinks slightly giving even more steps
next_move: if better, tune LRs for this config; if worse, 2-head 256-dim is optimal

replacements.old: HEAD_DIM = 128          # target head dimension for attention
replacements.new: HEAD_DIM = 64           # target head dimension for attention

description: change MLP from 4x to 6x hidden dimension expansion
hypothesis: depth-2 has fewer layers but with more steps; a wider MLP per layer might compensate for the lack of depth with more capacity per block
next_move: if better, depth-2 benefits from wider MLPs; if worse, 4x is well-calibrated

description: change n_kv_head from num_heads to 1 in build_model_config — multi-query attention with 1 shared KV head
hypothesis: MQA reduces KV parameters; the saved capacity and compute may allow more effective steps in the 5-min budget
next_move: if better, MQA suits the short-run regime; if worse, full per-head KV is needed
